fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: North Carolina Physical Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a physical therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to North Carolina - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: physical therapist license reciprocity north carolina, how to transfer physical therapist license to north carolina, physical therapist compact north carolina, north carolina physical therapist endorsement application

board.name: North Carolina Board of Physical Therapy Examiners
board.mailing_address:
NC Board of Physical Therapy Examiners
8300 Health Park, Suite 233
Raleigh, NC 27615

license_types.title: PT, DPT
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a physical therapist in North Carolina.

steps.title: Create Online Gateway Account and Submit Application
steps.description: Register on the NC Board of Physical Therapy Examiners' (NCBPTE) Online Applicant Gateway, create an account, and complete the online application for initial licensure or endorsement.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documentation
steps.description: Arrange for official transcripts from your physical therapy program to be sent directly to the Board. If applying by endorsement, provide verification of any out-of-state PT licenses held.

steps.title: Complete the Jurisprudence Exam
steps.description: Take and pass the North Carolina Jurisprudence Exam (NCJP), which is available online through the Online Applicant Gateway.

steps.title: Pass the National Physical Therapy Examination (NPTE)
steps.description: Register with the Federation of State Boards of Physical Therapy (FSBPT) and pass the NPTE. Ensure your scores are sent directly to the NCBPTE.

steps.title: Undergo a Criminal Background Check
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided within the Online Applicant Gateway to complete the required criminal background check.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination).

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NPTE. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a North Carolina physical therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: For endorsement applicants, the state licensing fee is $150. Additional third-party costs (fingerprinting, transcripts, or verification) may apply.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NPTE (National Physical Therapy Examination) is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does North Carolina have reciprocity for physical therapists?
faqs.answer: Yes. North Carolina participates in the PT Compact. If your home state and license qualify for compact privilege, you can typically practice in North Carolina without a separate endorsement application.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my physical therapist license to North Carolina?
faqs.answer: Current board processing time is 2 to 4 Weeks once your file is complete.

faqs.question: Can I start working in North Carolina while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Physical Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Physical Therapist in North Carolina
hero.dek: To apply for a Physical Therapist license in North Carolina, you must register and submit your application through the NC Board of Physical Therapy Examiners' Online Applicant Gateway.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Go to the NC Board of Physical Therapy Examiners' Online Applicant Gateway and click 'Don't have an account? Create an Account'. Follow the prompts to set up your account with a valid email and password, then log in and select the appropriate application type (e.g., Initial Licensure or Licensure by Endorsement).
